question_text: A student sets up a circuit with a battery, a light bulb, and an ammeter. Where should the ammeter be placed to measure the current flowing through the light bulb?

options.label: A
options.text: Connected in parallel across the light bulb

options.label: B
options.text: Connected in series in the circuit, so current flows through it

options.label: C
options.text: Connected to the positive terminal of the battery only

options.label: D
options.text: Placed next to the light bulb without being connected

explanation: An ammeter measures current and must be placed in series (in the path of the current) so that all the current flowing through the circuit passes through it. If connected in parallel, it would damage the ammeter because it has very low resistance.
